result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a Bayesian-driven adaptive search using spatial probability maps. This involves: 1. Initial sparse grid probing to identify potential seed locations. 2. Bayesian shape matching to calculate the posterior probability of various polyomino orientations and placements based on noisy aggregate query results. 3. Iterative boundary refinement where drilling is focused on high-uncertainty cells at the edges of high-probability clusters. 4. Final guess generation using the Maximum A Posteriori (MAP) estimate of the field configuration.

JUSTIFICATION: This approach minimizes probing cost by treating the search as an information-gain problem, using Bayesian inference to handle noisy aggregate data and reducing redundant drilling through probabilistic shape matching.
